DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS DANA DYE AND TODD DYE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-Defendants' (Dana Dye and Todd Dye (collectively 'The Dyes')) demurrer is overruled.

"A person against whom a complaint or cross-complaint has been filed may, within 30 days after service of the complaint or cross-complaint, demur to the complaint or cross-complaint." (CCP sec. 430.40.) The First Amended Cross Complaint was filed on May 19, 2021. Paul Fife, the attorney for the Dyes, signed a Notice and Acknowledgement of receipt of the First Amended Cross-Complaint on June 24, 2022. (Dyes' Index of Exhibits, Ex. F.) The Dyes did not file this Demurrer until November 28, 2022, over four months after the deadline.

The Dyes argue that a discretionary extension to the deadline is warranted because Transform California Builder Appliances LLC ('Transform') dodged and delayed meet and confer efforts, and Transform did not take steps to obtain a default judgment for months. (See Jackson v. Doe (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 742, 749 [trial court acted within its discretion in considering defendant's demurrer, which was filed eight days after the expiration of the 30-day time period].)

In his declaration, Fife supplies several emails with opposing counsel regarding meet and confer spanning, between October 4 and November 22. (Fife Decl., Ex. B-D.) Transform's index of exhibits includes an email dated September 29 regarding meet and confer. (Transform's Index, Ex. J.) Noticeably absent from either party's exhibits is proof of correspondence between June 24 and September 29, let alone correspondence showing that Transform attempted to delay the meet and confer process. The unexplained lengthy delay in filing this demurrer does not warrant an exercise of the court's discretion to overlook the 30-day filing deadline.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Stay Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Paul Fife (paulfife@fifelawllp.com) for Plaintiff.
Anthony Barron (abarron@nixonpeabody.com) and Conor McNamara (cmcnamara@nixonpeabody.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GILMAN SCREENS & KITCHENS, INC.'s Motion To Stay Arbitration is denied, and the litigation is stayed pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4.
